animal.name: animal.n.01
animal.definition: a living organism characterized by voluntary movement

children.name: migrator.n.02
children.definition: an animal (especially birds and fish) that travels between different habitats at particular times of the year

children.name: invertebrate.n.01
children.definition: any animal lacking a backbone or notochord; the term is not used as a scientific classification

children.name: entoproct.n.01
children.definition: any of various moss-like aquatic animals usually forming branching colonies; each polyp having a both mouth and anus within a closed ring of tentacles

children.name: coelenterate.n.01
children.definition: radially symmetrical animals having saclike bodies with only one opening and tentacles with stinging structures; they occur in polyp and medusa forms

children.name: ctenophore.n.01
children.definition: biradially symmetrical hermaphroditic solitary marine animals resembling jellyfishes having for locomotion eight rows of cilia arranged like teeth in a comb

children.name: woodborer.n.01
children.definition: any of various insects or larvae or mollusks that bore into wood

children.name: phoronid.n.01
children.definition: hermaphrodite wormlike animal living in mud of the sea bottom

children.name: mollusk.n.01
children.definition: invertebrate having a soft unsegmented body usually enclosed in a shell

children.name: zoophyte.n.01
children.definition: any of various invertebrate animals resembling a plant such as a sea anemone or coral or sponge

children.name: brachiopod.n.01
children.definition: marine animal with bivalve shell having a pair of arms bearing tentacles for capturing food; found worldwide

children.name: rotifer.n.01
children.definition: minute aquatic multicellular organisms having a ciliated wheel-like organ for feeding and locomotion; constituents of freshwater plankton

children.name: peanut_worm.n.01
children.definition: small unsegmented marine worm that when disturbed retracts its anterior portion into the body giving the appearance of a peanut

children.name: mutant.n.02
children.definition: an animal that has undergone mutation

children.name: omnivore.n.02
children.definition: an animal that feeds on both animal and vegetable substances

children.name: herbivore.n.01
children.definition: any animal that feeds chiefly on grass and other plants

children.name: game.n.04
children.definition: animal hunted for food or sport

children.name: big_game.n.01
children.definition: large animals that are hunted for sport

children.name: game_bird.n.01
children.definition: any bird (as grouse or pheasant) that is hunted for sport

children.name: prey.n.02
children.definition: animal hunted or caught for food

children.name: insectivore.n.02
children.definition: any organism that feeds mainly on insects

children.name: fictional_animal.n.01
children.definition: animals that exist only in fiction (usually in children's stories)

children.name: domestic_animal.n.01
children.definition: any of various animals that have been tamed and made fit for a human environment

children.name: stray.n.01
children.definition: an animal that has strayed (especially a domestic animal)

children.name: head.n.02
children.definition: a single domestic animal

children.name: stocker.n.01
children.definition: a domestic animal (especially a young steer or heifer) kept as stock until fattened or matured and suitable for a breeding establishment

children.name: domestic_cat.n.01
children.definition: any domesticated member of the genus Felis

children.name: dog.n.01
children.definition: a member of the genus Canis (probably descended from the common wolf) that has been domesticated by man since prehistoric times; occurs in many breeds

children.name: feeder.n.01
children.definition: an animal being fattened or suitable for fattening

plant.name: plant.n.02
plant.definition: (botany) a living organism lacking the power of locomotion

children.name: endemic.n.02
children.definition: a plant that is native to a certain limited area

children.name: annual.n.01
children.definition: (botany) a plant that completes its entire life cycle within the space of a year

children.name: plantlet.n.01
children.definition: a young plant or a small plant

children.name: myrmecophyte.n.01
children.definition: plant that affords shelter or food to ants that live in symbiotic relations with it

children.name: aquatic.n.01
children.definition: a plant that lives in or on water

children.name: escape.n.06
children.definition: a plant originally cultivated but now growing wild

children.name: gametophyte.n.01
children.definition: the gamete-bearing individual or phase in the life cycle of a plant having alternation of generations

children.name: microgametophyte.n.01
children.definition: the male gametophyte produced by a microspore

children.name: megagametophyte.n.01
children.definition: the female gametophyte produced by the megaspore of a plant that produces both microspore and megaspores

children.name: ornamental.n.01
children.definition: any plant grown for its beauty or ornamental value

children.name: flowering_maple.n.01
children.definition: an ornamental plant of the genus Abutilon having leaves that resemble maple leaves

children.name: sporophyte.n.01
children.definition: the spore-producing individual or phase in the life cycle of a plant having alternation of generations

children.name: wilding.n.01
children.definition: a wild uncultivated plant (especially a wild apple or crabapple tree)

children.name: wildflower.n.01
children.definition: wild or uncultivated flowering plant

vehicle.name: vehicle.n.01
vehicle.definition: a conveyance that transports people or objects

children.name: military_vehicle.n.01
children.definition: vehicle used by the armed forces

children.name: picket.n.04
children.definition: a vehicle performing sentinel duty

children.name: humvee.n.01
children.definition: a high mobility, multipurpose, military vehicle with four-wheel drive

children.name: half_track.n.01
children.definition: a motor vehicle propelled by half tracks; frequently used by the military

children.name: warplane.n.01
children.definition: an aircraft designed and used for combat

children.name: technical.n.01
children.definition: a pickup truck with a gun mounted on it

children.name: troop_carrier.n.01
children.definition: any land or sea or air vehicle designed to carry troops

children.name: personnel_carrier.n.01
children.definition: a military vehicle (usually armored) for transporting military personnel and their equipment

children.name: tank.n.01
children.definition: an enclosed armored military vehicle; has a cannon and moves on caterpillar treads

children.name: weapons_carrier.n.01
children.definition: military vehicle that is a light truck designed to carry mortars or machine guns and their crews

children.name: caisson.n.02
children.definition: a two-wheeled military vehicle carrying artillery ammunition

children.name: wheeled_vehicle.n.01
children.definition: a vehicle that moves on wheels and usually has a container for transporting things or people

children.name: car.n.02
children.definition: a wheeled vehicle adapted to the rails of railroad

children.name: handcart.n.01
children.definition: wheeled vehicle that can be pushed by a person; may have one or two or four wheels

children.name: self-propelled_vehicle.n.01
children.definition: a wheeled vehicle that carries in itself a means of propulsion

children.name: wagon.n.04
children.definition: a child's four-wheeled toy cart sometimes used for coasting

children.name: scooter.n.02
children.definition: child's two-wheeled vehicle operated by foot

children.name: wagon.n.01
children.definition: any of various kinds of wheeled vehicles drawn by an animal or a tractor

children.name: rolling_stock.n.01
children.definition: collection of wheeled vehicles owned by a railroad or motor carrier

children.name: unicycle.n.01
children.definition: a vehicle with a single wheel that is driven by pedals

children.name: skateboard.n.01
children.definition: a board with wheels that is ridden in a standing or crouching position and propelled by foot

children.name: bicycle.n.01
children.definition: a wheeled vehicle that has two wheels and is moved by foot pedals

children.name: rocket.n.01
children.definition: any vehicle self-propelled by a rocket engine

children.name: missile.n.01
children.definition: a rocket carrying a warhead of conventional or nuclear explosives; may be ballistic or directed by remote control

children.name: test_rocket.n.01
children.definition: a rocket fired for test purposes

children.name: multistage_rocket.n.01
children.definition: a rocket having two or more rocket engines (each with its own fuel) that are fired in succession and jettisoned when the fuel is exhausted

children.name: bumper_car.n.01
children.definition: a small low-powered electrically powered vehicle driven on a special platform where there are many others to be dodged

children.name: steamroller.n.02
children.definition: vehicle equipped with heavy wide smooth rollers for compacting roads and pavements

children.name: craft.n.02
children.definition: a vehicle designed for navigation in or on water or air or through outer space

children.name: spacecraft.n.01
children.definition: a craft capable of traveling in outer space; technically, a satellite around the sun

children.name: aircraft.n.01
children.definition: a vehicle that can fly

children.name: landing_craft.n.01
children.definition: naval craft designed for putting ashore troops and equipment

children.name: hovercraft.n.01
children.definition: a craft capable of moving over water or land on a cushion of air created by jet engines

children.name: vessel.n.02
children.definition: a craft designed for water transportation

children.name: skibob.n.01
children.definition: a vehicle resembling a bicycle but having skis instead of wheels; the rider wears short skis for balancing

children.name: sled.n.01
children.definition: a vehicle mounted on runners and pulled by horses or dogs; for transportation over snow

children.name: luge.n.01
children.definition: a racing sled for one or two people

children.name: bobsled.n.02
children.definition: a long racing sled (for 2 or more people) with a steering mechanism

children.name: toboggan.n.01
children.definition: a long narrow sled without runners; boards curve upward in front

children.name: bobsled.n.01
children.definition: formerly two short sleds coupled together

children.name: dogsled.n.01
children.definition: a sled pulled by dogs

children.name: pung.n.01
children.definition: a one-horse sleigh consisting of a box on runners

food.name: food.n.01
food.definition: any substance that can be metabolized by an animal to give energy and build tissue

children.name: micronutrient.n.01
children.definition: a substance needed only in small amounts for normal body function (e.g., vitamins or minerals)

children.name: foodstuff.n.02
children.definition: a substance that can be used or prepared for use as food

children.name: juice.n.01
children.definition: the liquid part that can be extracted from plant or animal tissue by squeezing or cooking

children.name: egg.n.02
children.definition: oval reproductive body of a fowl (especially a hen) used as food

children.name: coloring.n.01
children.definition: a digestible substance used to give color to food

children.name: concoction.n.01
children.definition: any foodstuff made by combining different ingredients

children.name: canned_food.n.01
children.definition: food preserved by canning

children.name: ingredient.n.03
children.definition: food that is a component of a mixture in cooking

children.name: dairy_product.n.01
children.definition: milk and butter and cheese

children.name: flour.n.01
children.definition: fine powdery foodstuff obtained by grinding and sifting the meal of a cereal grain

children.name: grain.n.02
children.definition: foodstuff prepared from the starchy grains of cereal grasses

children.name: cocoa.n.02
children.definition: powder of ground roasted cacao beans with most of the fat removed

children.name: beverage.n.01
children.definition: any liquid suitable for drinking

children.name: near_beer.n.01
children.definition: drink that resembles beer but with less than 1/2 percent alcohol

children.name: refresher.n.02
children.definition: a drink that refreshes

children.name: cooler.n.02
children.definition: an iced drink especially white wine and fruit juice

children.name: smoothie.n.02
children.definition: a thick smooth drink consisting of fresh fruit pureed with ice cream or yoghurt or milk

children.name: wish-wash.n.01
children.definition: any thin watery drink

children.name: coffee.n.01
children.definition: a beverage consisting of an infusion of ground coffee beans

children.name: cocoa.n.01
children.definition: a beverage made from cocoa powder and milk and sugar; usually drunk hot

children.name: soft_drink.n.01
children.definition: nonalcoholic beverage (usually carbonated)

children.name: fruit_juice.n.01
children.definition: drink produced by squeezing or crushing fruit

children.name: mate.n.09
children.definition: South American tea-like drink made from leaves of a South American holly called mate

children.name: chyme.n.01
children.definition: a semiliquid mass of partially digested food that passes from the stomach through the pyloric sphincter into the duodenum

children.name: commissariat.n.01
children.definition: a stock or supply of foods

children.name: food_cache.n.01
children.definition: food in a secure or hidden storage place

children.name: larder.n.01
children.definition: a supply of food especially for a household

children.name: feed.n.01
children.definition: food for domestic livestock

children.name: mash.n.02
children.definition: mixture of ground animal feeds

children.name: cattle_cake.n.01
children.definition: a concentrated feed for cattle; processed in the form of blocks or cakes

children.name: silage.n.01
children.definition: fodder harvested while green and kept succulent by partial fermentation as in a silo

children.name: slop.n.01
children.definition: wet feed (especially for pigs) consisting of mostly kitchen waste mixed with water or skimmed or sour milk

children.name: bird_feed.n.01
children.definition: food given to birds; usually mixed seeds

children.name: fodder.n.02
children.definition: coarse food (especially for livestock) composed of entire plants or the leaves and stalks of a cereal crop

children.name: blood_meal.n.01
children.definition: the dried and powdered blood of animals

children.name: corn_gluten_feed.n.01
children.definition: a feed consisting primarily of corn gluten

children.name: feed_grain.n.01
children.definition: grain grown for cattle feed

children.name: oil_cake.n.01
children.definition: mass of e.g. linseed or cottonseed or soybean from which the oil has been pressed; used as food for livestock

children.name: water.n.06
children.definition: a liquid necessary for the life of most animals and plants

children.name: branch_water.n.01
children.definition: pure natural water from a stream or brook; often distinguished from soda water

children.name: spring_water.n.02
children.definition: water from a spring

children.name: drinking_water.n.01
children.definition: water suitable for drinking

children.name: culture_medium.n.01
children.definition: (bacteriology) a nutrient substance (solid or liquid) that is used to cultivate micro-organisms

children.name: agar.n.01
children.definition: any culture medium that uses agar as the gelling agent

children.name: nutriment.n.01
children.definition: a source of materials to nourish the body

children.name: stodge.n.01
children.definition: heavy and filling (and usually starchy) food

children.name: ingesta.n.01
children.definition: solid and liquid nourishment taken into the body through the mouth

children.name: course.n.07
children.definition: part of a meal served at one time

children.name: milk.n.02
children.definition: produced by mammary glands of female mammals for feeding their young

children.name: vitamin.n.01
children.definition: any of a group of organic substances essential in small quantities to normal metabolism

children.name: fast_food.n.01
children.definition: inexpensive food (hamburgers or chicken or milkshakes) prepared and served quickly

children.name: mess.n.03
children.definition: soft semiliquid food

children.name: wheat_germ.n.01
children.definition: embryo of the wheat kernel; removed before milling and eaten as a source of vitamins

children.name: finger_food.n.01
children.definition: food to be eaten with the fingers

children.name: dainty.n.01
children.definition: something considered choice to eat

children.name: yolk.n.02
children.definition: nutritive material of an ovum stored for the nutrition of an embryo (especially the yellow mass of a bird or reptile egg)

color.name: color.n.01
color.definition: a visual attribute of things that results from the light they emit or transmit or reflect

children.name: chromatic_color.n.01
children.definition: a color that has hue

children.name: blond.n.02
children.definition: a light grayish yellow to near white

children.name: salmon.n.04
children.definition: a pale pinkish orange color

children.name: pink.n.01
children.definition: a light shade of red

children.name: complementary_color.n.01
children.definition: either one of two chromatic colors that when mixed together give white (in the case of lights) or grey (in the case of pigments)

children.name: purple.n.01
children.definition: a purple color or pigment

children.name: pastel.n.01
children.definition: any of various pale or light colors

children.name: blue.n.01
children.definition: blue color or pigment; resembling the color of the clear sky in the daytime

children.name: yellow.n.01
children.definition: yellow color or pigment; the chromatic color resembling the hue of sunflowers or ripe lemons

children.name: green.n.01
children.definition: green color or pigment; resembling the color of growing grass

children.name: orange.n.02
children.definition: orange color or pigment; any of a range of colors between red and yellow

children.name: complexion.n.01
children.definition: the coloring of a person's face

children.name: paleness.n.02
children.definition: the property of having a naturally light complexion

children.name: tawniness.n.01
children.definition: the quality or state of being the color of tanned leather

children.name: lividness.n.01
children.definition: unnatural lack of color in the skin (as from bruising or sickness or emotional distress)

children.name: whiteness.n.03
children.definition: lightness or fairness of complexion

children.name: sallowness.n.01
children.definition: a sickly yellowish skin color

children.name: darkness.n.06
children.definition: a swarthy complexion

children.name: ruddiness.n.01
children.definition: a healthy reddish complexion

children.name: coloration.n.02
children.definition: appearance with regard to color

children.name: protective_coloration.n.01
children.definition: coloration making an organism less visible or attractive to predators

children.name: hair_coloring.n.02
children.definition: coloring of the hair

children.name: pigmentation.n.02
children.definition: coloration of living tissues by pigment

children.name: depigmentation.n.01
children.definition: absence or loss of pigmentation (or less than normal pigmentation) in the skin or hair

children.name: primary_color.n.01
children.definition: any of three colors from which all others can be obtained by mixing

children.name: primary_color_for_pigments.n.01
children.definition: any of three pigments from which all colors can be obtained by mixing

children.name: primary_color_for_light.n.01
children.definition: any of three primary colors of light from which all colors can be obtained by additive mixing

children.name: shade.n.02
children.definition: a quality of a given color that differs slightly from another color

children.name: undertone.n.03
children.definition: a pale or subdued color

children.name: mellowness.n.04
children.definition: a soft shade of a color

children.name: richness.n.06
children.definition: a strong deep vividness of hue

children.name: achromatic_color.n.01
children.definition: a color lacking hue; white or grey or black

children.name: white.n.02
children.definition: the quality or state of the achromatic color of greatest lightness (bearing the least resemblance to black)

children.name: gray.n.01
children.definition: a neutral achromatic color midway between white and black

children.name: black.n.01
children.definition: the quality or state of the achromatic color of least lightness (bearing the least resemblance to white)

children.name: nonsolid_color.n.01
children.definition: a color produced by a pattern of differently colored dots that together simulate the desired color

children.name: heather_mixture.n.01
children.definition: interwoven yarns of mixed colors producing muted greyish shades with flecks of color

children.name: mottle.n.01
children.definition: an irregular arrangement of patches of color
